title: Warm up
content: Three images depict taxi drivers navigating different Chinese cities from inside a car: Beijing with modern skyline and traffic, Chongqing with mountainous traditional architecture and a passenger reading, and Hangzhou with a lakeside view and smart technology interface. The slide introduces the theme of exploring China through the perspective of taxi drivers in different cities.
key_claims: Beijing scene shows modern city traffic and skyscrapers alongside traditional architecture
Chongqing scene shows mountainous terrain with traditional buildings and a cable car
Hangzhou scene shows a lake view with smart phone/AI technology integration in the vehicle

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This title slide introduces the presentation's theme 'A Ride Through China' using images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture including the Forbidden City, illuminated traditional buildings, and a pagoda. It sets up the metaphor of a taxi ride to explore China's development.
key_claims: Presentation subtitle is 'A Ride Through China'
Visual imagery combines taxi symbolism with traditional Chinese architectural landmarks

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines a three-step investigative framework for analyzing Chinese cities, illustrated with an isometric city graphic. The process flows from identifying Local Features to determining Development Strategies to evaluating Outcomes & Positive Impact.
key_claims: Investigation framework consists of three sequential steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact
The process is designed as a chain where each stage leads to the next

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This matching exercise slide pairs three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) with their corresponding development strategies. Lines connect each city to a strategy, with accompanying images showing the Forbidden City, illuminated buildings, and a pagoda.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Sustainable urban development
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合)

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide synthesizes the taxi driver perspective images with their correct development strategy labels, correcting the matching from the previous slide. It concludes with the overarching theme that China is an evolving nation where each city tailors strategies to its local features, referencing the Chinese principle 因地制宜 (adapting to local conditions).
key_claims: Beijing's strategy is sustainable urban development
Chongqing's strategy is tourism & economic growth
Hangzhou's strategy is technology & tradition integration
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜)
